rationale_for_baseline_tier: A warm, fluid-filled knee with low-grade fever is a 'hot joint' that must be aspirated to exclude septic arthritis, and joints cannot be examined or aspirated via telehealth. Although dactylitis, nail pitting and the asymmetric pattern strongly favour PsA (and there is no fiery/tense joint or rigors), the hot joint mandates urgent same-day in-person assessment (± aspiration) (T3). T3 is also the minimum viable pass, since phone-only reassurance for a hot joint would under-triage. Escalate to T5 for a fiery/tense hot knee or high fever with rigors.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Knee becoming fiery red, tense and unbearably hot, or a high fever with shaking chills
escalation_edges.rationale: Would strongly indicate septic arthritis/sepsis needing emergency aspiration and antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your knee becomes fiery red, tense and unbearably hot, or you get a high fever with shaking chills, go to ED or call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to prepare food or mobilise safely with the dominant hand and knee affected
escalation_edges.rationale: Impaired function while isolated needs practical support and timely assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't prepare food or move safely because of your hand and knee, arrange same-day help along with the joint assessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with dominant-hand and knee involvement
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Arrange practical support and offloading aids alongside the urgent joint assessment.
Refer to rheumatology for PsA management once infection is excluded.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: The sausage finger and nail pitting point to psoriatic arthritis flaring, but your warm, fluid-filled knee has to be checked in person today and may need to be drained to rule out a joint infection. Rest and elevate the joints, take simple painkillers within limits, and arrange to be seen urgently the same day.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person joint assessment (± aspiration); use ED for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use ED for a fiery/tense hot knee or high fever with rigors; otherwise arrange urgent same-day assessment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the dactylitis + warm knee effusion with nail pitting, low-grade fever, and that knee aspiration is needed to exclude septic arthritis.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Managing/reassuring a warm effused joint remotely without aspiration, missing septic arthritis.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A septic knee is not aspirated; the joint is destroyed and the patient becomes septic.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Immediate ambulance/000 when urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3/T4) would suffice for a warm-but-not-fulminant joint.
